faqs.q: What is the difference between hypomania and mania?
faqs.a: Hypomania is a milder, shorter version of mania. A hypomanic episode lasts at least four days and isn't severe enough to seriously disrupt daily life, require hospital care, or involve a loss of touch with reality, while mania is more intense and can cause major disruption and psychotic symptoms.

faqs.q: How long does a hypomanic episode last?
faqs.a: A hypomanic episode lasts at least four days in a row and is a clear change from a person's usual state. The symptoms stay noticeable but don't reach the severity of full mania.

faqs.q: Why is hypomania easy to miss?
faqs.a: Because hypomania can feel good and may even boost productivity for a while, it's often mistaken for simply having a great stretch of days. Spotting the pattern, especially when it swings into depression, matters for getting the right diagnosis and care.
